title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two Merrimac officers rescued a sleeping woman from a house fire early Wednesday morning on Bear Hill Road.
Officers Paul DeCoste and Adam White found heavy smoke and flames upon arriving at the scene around 5:30 a.m.
Resident Jeff Strogney called 911 after seeing 10-15 foot flames, alerting police to the emergency situation.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old Marshfield man, Mathew McTiernan, died in a three-car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The accident occurred at 6:20 a.m. Wednesday, affecting two of the three traffic lanes, per the Massachusetts State Police.
State police from the Milton Barracks are investigating the cause of the crash that involved a Toyota sedan.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: Baby boomers are increasingly burdened by rising housing costs, with a 70% rent hike reported at a Southbridge housing complex.
The elderly segment of the Massachusetts population has doubled since 2010, complicating housing needs with complex aging requirements.
Housing complexes like Southbridge's Lebanon Hill face challenges with rising costs and lack of federal subsidies, affecting senior residents.

title: You can now pass through TSA PreCheck at Logan using only your face. Should that make us nervous?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Logan Airport introduced TSA's Touchless ID, allowing PreCheck travelers to use facial recognition instead of physical IDs.
The program, operational in Terminals A, B, and E, aims to reduce wait times, but raises privacy concerns.
Critics warn about potential mass surveillance and lack of statutory protection for biometric data collected by TSA.

title: Kraft Group, Foxborough announce World Cup funding deal for Gillette Stadium security
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Foxborough, Kraft Sports & Entertainment, and Boston Soccer 26 reached a funding agreement for World Cup security at Gillette Stadium.
The agreement ensures Foxborough incurs no financial burden, with Boston Soccer 2026 covering $7.8 million in security costs.
Seven World Cup games, including Haiti vs. Scotland on June 13, are scheduled for Gillette Stadium this summer.

title: Natick mom to run the 2026 Boston Marathon for kinder communities
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Danica Champion, Natick ESL teacher, will run her first marathon in 2026 to support Spark Kindness, Inc.
Spark Kindness promotes values like kindness, justice, and empathy, enhancing youth and community health outcomes.
Champion has run half-marathons before; funds raised will aid programs addressing bullying and discrimination.

title: 10 predictions for the Red Sox this season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet is predicted to win the Cy Young Award after finishing second last year.
Trevor Story is expected to improve his defensive skills, reducing errors at shortstop.
The Red Sox are predicted to finish 92-70, winning the wild-card series but losing in the ALDS.

title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill plans 26-story tower in Davis Square, Somerville, with 502-unit apartment including 126 affordable units.
After community backlash, developer pledged to present new project options and to open a project office in March.
Proposed construction could temporarily affect businesses like The Burren, Dragon Pizza, and McKinnon's Meat Market.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his nephews with Down syndrome, Sebastian and Zachary.
Ponte's family received support from the Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress after the birth of his nephews.
Sebastian passed away, but Ponte runs to support families facing challenges like his through MDSC programs.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
The drone strike in Kuwait killed six U.S. soldiers; Garcia is recovering in Germany, reported by Gov. Maura Healey.
Garcia is part of Engine 50 in Charlestown; Boston Firefighters IAFF Local 718 expressed pride in his service.
